subject: recording process
object: three takes minimum
text: You always record a video three times over, and this is for three simple reasons.

subject: first two takes
object: performance pressure
text: knowing that you record three times removes all the pressure from the first and the second recording, making you look and sound more natural because you know in the back of your head that you always have the third

subject: third take
object: natural delivery from repetition
text: by the time you actually get to your third recording, you've already said everything you're going to say so many times that you already know exactly how you want everything to come out

subject: editor
object: all three versions
text: you or your editor have three or more clips from each part of your video so the editor can watch all three versions and pick the best parts from each version and combine them into the best possible recording

subject: extra effort
object: competitive advantage most skip
text: it is going to add 10 minutes onto your recording process, but it is what most people won't do. This is how you can get ahead

subject: tired-of hook
object: ongoing frustration
text: Are you tired of not being able to lose your stubborn belly fat?

subject: caution hook
object: threat of mistake
text: If you're trying to lose that stubborn belly fat so that way you look good for summer, then you absolutely need to stay away from this one food.

subject: regret hook
object: creator vulnerability
text: I regret all those years doing those stupid fad diets just to lose that last little bit of belly fat whenever all I had to do was this one thing.

subject: call-out hook
object: physical self-identification
text: If you can take your hand and grab onto your belly fat, then you're going to want to watch this video.

subject: one content idea

text: Here's how to transform one shitty content idea into 5 viral hooks for your next video

subject: standard CTAs (follow me, like, save)
object: average watch time and video ranking

subject: loop ending
object: watch time and algorithm ranking
text: with a loop your viewers never know whenever a video is going to end. And then when it does, it loops the viewer back to the beginning of your video, which will increase watch time, hence your video will rank higher in the algorithm

subject: seamless loop
object: splitting hook sentence at a comma and moving first half to end
text: the easiest way to create a seamless loop is by going over to your hook and then placing a comma in front of it and then coming up with a phrase that makes it seem as if it's in the same sentence

subject: random CTA at video end
object: loop unless CTA has specific purpose
text: if you just create a random CTA that you felt like ending your video with, then you should not

subject: walking during capture phase

subject: construct phase

subject: condense phase

subject: CTA at end of video
object: video has no more value and they should scroll

subject: CTA at beginning of video
object: viewer has received no value to act on yet

subject: CTA placement
object: second-to-last step so value follows and trust is established
text: You always put your CTA on the second to last step. So if your video has five steps, do not put your CTA before step one or after step five. You always place it right before step five

subject: second-to-last CTA placement
object: prior value delivery builds trust that the CTA offer is worth it
text: your viewers don't just watch your CTA. They actually are going to act on it because they trust it is worth it. So as long as steps one through four was good s**t, whenever you say save this video, they actually will because they now know that it is worth it

object: free additional views on Reels
text: go to settings, and then cross-posting, then under automatically share, tap next to your Instagram reels. Now, your videos will get views from Facebook

subject: account suggestions setting
object: competitor accounts
text: go to edit profile on desktop, and then make sure account suggestions is turned on. This will get your profile suggested on your competitor's accounts

subject: trial reel strategy
object: views by targeting non-followers separately
text: the trial reel will only be shown to new people that don't follow you. So same video, but you get double the views all for free

subject: duplicate draft method
object: posting same video as both trial and normal reel
text: save it as a draft. Then open your drafts tap on the three dots next to it and duplicate it. Now open the first draft and make it a trial reel and then hit post and then you're gonna go back and you're gonna post the second draft as a normal reel

subject: visual production quality
object: hooks can grab attention regardless of quality
text: it's not only about what you say or how you say it, it's also about who's saying it

subject: professional lighting, background, and camera
object: hook effectiveness
text: if I swap my professional lighting for the room lights, and if I swap my $6,000 camera for my iPhone, then I'll never be able to grab your attention no matter how good my hooks might be

subject: great hooks
object: visual presentation is low quality
text: I'll never be able to grab your attention no matter how good my hooks might be. Because it's not only about what you say or how you say it, it's also about who's saying it

subject: pinned comment hook strategy
object: comment on own reel to add curiosity or context unavailable in caption
text: Pin a comment on your reel. This is different from your caption. A pinned comment shows up right below your video and it's the first thing people see when they open the comments.

subject: pinned comment
object: DM opt-in for a resource using keyword trigger
text: Drop an emoji in the comments and I'll send you the full list. Something like drop a fire emoji and I'll DM you the swipe file.

subject: pinned comment
object: algorithm to push video further
text: When people reply to your pinned comment, that counts as engagement. More comments means the algorithm pushes your video to more people.

subject: headline testing workflow
object: email subject line A/B testing before committing to reel hook
text: Before you film your hook, test it as an email subject line first. Send two versions to your list and whichever one gets a higher open rate, that's your hook.

subject: hook testing via email
object: email open rates are the fastest proxy for curiosity without production cost
text: Email subject lines and video hooks follow the same psychology. If someone opens an email for a subject line, they would have watched the video for that same hook.

subject: Edits app analytics
object: hooks with lowest skip rates to prioritize
text: inside the edits app, if you open up your analytics at the bottom right, then in the middle, scroll across until you find lowest skip rates, Instagram will show you the best hooks you've ever posted

subject: sub-50% skip rate hooks
object: go viral even if they didn't the first time
text: Anything below a 50% skip rate means that your hook has the potential to go viral even if it didn't the first time

subject: ChatGPT hook template prompt
object: best past hooks into reusable viral hook templates
text: paste in your hooks and in seconds, it'll turn each one into a viral hook template, making it super easy to reuse all of your best hooks for every video you create moving forward

subject: content format multiplication
object: one idea into five viral videos using different formats
text: Here's how to turn one shitty content idea into five viral videos

subject: viral video formats
object: tier list, clone, green screen, and props formats
text: You can do a tier list format... You can do a clone... You can do a green screen... You can use props

subject: content format variation
object: reach from a single content idea
text: Let's say the idea for your video is picking the best workout plan. You can do a tier list format... You can do a clone... You can do a green screen... You can use props
